title: Pungent Smoky Greek Bryndza Cheese Pilaf
description: A smoky and silky Greek dish featuring bryndza cheese, this pilaf is perfect for a monsoon evening, with a dash-friendly flavor profile that will leave you wanting more. The combination of smoky and pungent flavors creates a truly unique taste experience.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the smoked paprika and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the uncooked white rice and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth, salt, and black pepper, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed.
Stir in the crumbled bryndza cheese until melted and well combined.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
